Editors

How to find video editor jobs

Finding better editing work is less about one job board and more about matching your search, portfolio, and outreach to the format teams need.

Use specific search terms

Search beyond the broad phrase video editor jobs. Try remote video editor jobs, YouTube video editor jobs, assistant video editor jobs, freelance video editor jobs, podcast editor, short-form editor, social media editor, and local city searches when you can work on-site.

Check communities before listings

Many early opportunities appear as questions, referrals, or vague help requests in Reddit threads, Facebook groups, Discords, creator communities, and local film groups. Reply with useful context first, then share matched proof when it fits the rules.

Build a fast proof packet

Keep a short profile ready with your best formats, two or three matched clips, software, timezone, rate range, weekly capacity, turnaround, and availability. The easier you are to forward, the more referrals can turn into real conversations.

Qualify the opportunity

Before applying, look for budget, deliverables, source footage volume, revision process, software, deadline, and references. If a post is missing those details, ask a clarifying question before investing time.
